SSL/TLS Grade Report
No Domain Name Grade No of Vulnerabilities Days Left for Expiry Expiry Date Issue Date
1 amazon.com B 2 146 Jan 7, 2025 Feb 1, 2024
2 time.is B 1 76 Oct 29, 2024 Jul 31, 2024
3 123.com B 0 66 Oct 19, 2024 Jul 21, 2024
4 w3schools.com A 1 259 Apr 30, 2025 Mar 31, 2024
5 www.w3schools.com A+ 1 263 May 4, 2025 Apr 2, 2024
6 cliq.com A+ 0 11 Aug 25, 2024 May 27, 2024
7 logs.com A+ 1 26 Sep 9, 2024 Jun 11, 2024
8 cricbuzz.com A+ 1 245 Apr 16, 2025 Mar 17, 2024
9 isc.org A+ 1 231 Apr 3, 2025 Mar 1, 2024
10 onlinesbi.com A+ 1 75 Oct 28, 2024 Sep 27, 2023
Grade

The SSL Certificate Grade calculations are done based on the supported protocols, supported cipher, certificate key exchange size, and certificate vulnerabilities. We support the following grades:

Grade Values
A+ 90 and above
A 80 to 90
B+ 75 to 80
B 65 to 75
C 50 to 65
D 35 to 50
E 20 to 35
F Below 20

Additional conditions for assigning the grades

  If the SSL certificate supports protocol version SSLv2, then the grade will be F.
  If the certificate has Freak or Logjam Vulnerability, then the grade will be F.
  If the certificate supports Export cipher, then the grade will be F.
  If the certificate does not support protocol version TLSv1.3, then the grade will be B.
  If the certificate does not support protocol version TLSv1.2, then the grade will be C.
  If the certificate supports protocol version SSLv3, the grade will be C.
  If the certificate is vulnerable to Poodle, then the grade will be C.
  If the certificate is vulnerable to RC4, then the grade will be B.
  If the certificate supports protocol versions TLSv1.0 or TLSv1.1, then the grade will be B.
payload.subject: SSL/TLS Grade Report
